title: B1 Listening - Part 1 - Test 7
instructions: For each question, listen to the recording and choose the correct answer: A, B or C.

items.context: You hear two people arranging a family picnic.
items.audio_script: Man: Are we still meeting on the beach for the family picnic? I thought I remembered correctly, but I have not checked my messages today. || Woman: That was the original plan, but it will be too windy beside the sea tomorrow. [1]We need to meet in the forest clearing instead[/1]. || Man: That was lucky; I almost went there. I would have gone to the wrong place and wondered where everyone was. || Woman: Do not worry. I will arrive ten minutes early, so look for me near the entrance. Keep your phone on in case anything changes again.
items.question: Where will the picnic be?
items.explanation: The picnic will be in the forest.

items.context: You hear two students preparing for a school play.
items.audio_script: Girl: Have you packed for the school play rehearsal yet? I put your script in my bag because I thought that was what we needed. || Boy: That was on the first list, but the teacher has printed spare scripts but wants everyone in costume. [1]Everyone needs to bring a black T-shirt now[/1]. || Girl: I am glad I checked. I was about to leave my bag by the door and forget about it. || Boy: There is still time. Put it in now, and check the final message once more before we leave tomorrow morning.
items.question: What should the student bring?
items.explanation: The student needs to bring a black T-shirt.

items.context: You hear two friends making birthday plans.
items.audio_script: Boy: Are we still having dinner at home for your birthday on Friday? I told my sister I would be home later, so I need to know. || Girl: That was the idea, but your parents have arranged a surprise meal for Saturday instead. [1]We are going to go ice skating instead[/1]. || Boy: That is fine with me. I nearly paid for the other activity online this morning. || Girl: It is lucky that you checked first. I will book our places this evening and send you the time. We can meet at the bus stop after lunch.
items.question: What will they do on Friday?

items.context: You hear two people talking about a swimming lesson.
items.audio_script: Woman: I need to write down the time for the swimming lesson. Is it still at quarter past four, like you said yesterday? || Man: No, the children's class is using the pool for longer today. [1]It starts at five o'clock now[/1], although we should arrive ten minutes earlier. || Woman: Thanks; I nearly wrote down the wrong time. I would have been waiting there at the wrong time. || Man: I will send you a reminder later. Bring everything you need, because there will not be time to go home again before it begins.
items.question: What time is the swimming lesson?
items.explanation: The lesson starts at five o'clock.

items.context: You hear two people talking about a printer.
items.audio_script: Man: How are you getting on with the printer at home? I heard it was not working properly. || Woman: I first thought it had run out of paper, and my brother wondered if the ink was empty. But he checked it carefully last night. [1]It cannot connect to the computer[/1]. || Man: Can it be fixed before your homework deadline tonight? || Woman: I think so. We know what is wrong now, and the repair shop said it should be ready tomorrow afternoon. That is a relief because I need it this weekend.
items.question: What is wrong with the printer?

options.C: It cannot connect to the computer

items.explanation: The printer cannot connect to the computer.

items.context: You hear two people planning a trip to the airport.
items.audio_script: Girl: Are you still travelling to the airport by train? I could meet you when you arrive. || Boy: I planned to, but there will be a rail strike tomorrow morning. I considered going by car, but nobody can drive us that early. [1]I am going by taxi instead[/1]. || Girl: That sounds like the best plan. What time will you get there? || Boy: Just before eleven, if everything is on time. I have already checked the timetable online, so it should be easy. I will message you when I leave, so you do not have to wait outside.
items.question: How will they get to the airport?
items.explanation: They decide to take a taxi.

items.context: You hear two people talking about a new dog.
items.audio_script: Boy: Did you buy a toy ball for the new dog? I saw one in the shop window yesterday and thought it looked nice. || Girl: No. I looked at a toy ball, and I nearly chose a food bowl, but it already has toys and bowls, but nowhere comfortable to sleep. [1]I bought a small bed in the end[/1]. || Boy: I think that was the right choice. It sounds more useful, and I think it will be appreciated. || Girl: I hope so. I kept the receipt, just in case it needs changing after we give it.
items.question: What did the speaker buy for the dog?
items.explanation: The speaker bought a small bed.
